AGI, Artificial
General Intelligence
is an exciting concept.

I think some of the
confusion around it

stems from the use

As you know, AI is a
general purpose technology,

meaning that it is useful
for many different things.

The rise of large
language models
has led to single models like
ChatGPT that are useful for

could be general purpose.

But a general purpose
technology is not

the same thing as artificial
general intelligence.

Let's take a look
at what really is

The most widely accepted
definition of AGI is AI.

They could do any intellectual
task that a human can.
Some definitions actually say

any tasks that a

but I'll stick with
this one for now.
For example, if we have AGI,

AI would be able
to learn to drive

20 hours of practice,
similar to what a teenager can.
This is an example raised by

Today, self driving cars

as 20 hours of practice.

the intellectual
task of completing

a PhD thesis level research

some parts of
brainstorming and writing
and maybe be a thought partner

But we're clearly very
far away from this still.

would also be able to do

knowledge workers

to carry out intellectual tasks.

Clearly, we're still
very far away from this.

different views about

how long it would

I think we're still
many decades away,

maybe even longer,
but I hope we'll

get there sometime
in a lifetimes.

that have made

if that's the definition of AGI,

be very valuable

technical breakthroughs

One of the hard things about

benchmarks artificial
intelligence

Artificial intelligence and

But to ask AI to do everything,

that's still just

But even though we're
